input_dict.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

visible_c_inputs.question: In which year was the album released which included the single "Long Kiss Goodnight", which was used as a sample in the song "Rap name"?
visible_c_inputs.summary_1:
First-hop evidence: Passage 1 — "'Long Kiss Goodnight' ... from the album 'Life After Death'."  
Bridge needed: The album "Life After Death" — confirm its release year.  
Retrieval direction: 1. Fetch the page titled "Life After Death (The Notorious B.I.G. album)" and inspect the infobox 'Released' / lead for the release date; 2. If needed, run the query "Life After Death release date site:wikipedia.org" to verify the year.

current_output.query: Life After Death (The Notorious B.I.G. album) release date site:wikipedia.org

output.query: Life After Death (The Notorious B.I.G. album) release date site:wikipedia.org

retrieved_doc_snippets: Life After Death | Life After Death is the second and final studio album by American rapper The Notorious B.I.G., released on March 25, 1997, on Bad Boy Records and Arista Records. A double album, it was released posthumously following his death on March 9, 1997. It features collaborations with guest artists such as 112, Jay-Z, Lil' Kim, Mase, Bone Thugs-n-Harmony, Too $hort, Angela Winbush, D.M.C. of Run-D.M.C. , R. Kelly, The LOX and Puff Daddy. "Life After Death" exhibits The Notorious B.I.G. ...[truncated]

Wikidumper.org | WikiDumper.org is a website created by Clifford A. Pickover that promises to permanently record a snapshot of the "best of the Wikipedia rejects", articles that are slated for deletion at the site. WikiDumper was launched on November 4, 2006, and accepts user submissions. Although the site doesn't specify its criteria for inclusion, many of its articles don't cite their sources. The site has been criticized as likely to be less accurate than Wikipedia.

Hypnotize (The Notorious B.I.G. song) | "Hypnotize" is a single by American rapper The Notorious B.I.G., released as the first single from his album "Life After Death" on March 1, 1997 and the last song released in his lifetime, as he was killed in a drive-by shooting a week later. It was the fifth song by a credited artist to hit number one posthumously. It was ranked at number 30 on "Rolling Stone"' s 50 greatest rap songs of all time in hip hop history.

Life or Death (C-Murder album) | Life or Death is the debut album by American rapper C-Murder by No Limit Records on March 17, 1998, It was produced by Beats By the Pound. Like most of No Limit's albums in the 1990s, the album was a success, peaking at #3 on the "Billboard" 200 and #1 on the Top R&B/Hip-Hop Albums, as well as #21 on the Top Heatseekers. After debuting on the charts with 197,000 copies sold its first week out, "Life or Death" quickly achieved Platinum status moving over 1 million ...[truncated]

Sky's the Limit (The Notorious B.I.G. song) | "Sky's the Limit" is the third single from The Notorious B.I.G.'s second album "Life After Death". It features vocals from R&B group 112 and somber production from Clark Kent. It contains a sample from the songs "Is This Love" by Whitesnake, "My Flame" by Bobby Caldwell and "Keep On" by D. Train. In the US, it was released as a triple A-side along with "Going Back to Cali" and "Kick In the Door". The track was certified Gold by the RIAA in 1998.

Ready to Die | Ready to Die is the debut studio album by American rapper The Notorious B.I.G.; it was released on September 13, 1994, by Bad Boy Records and Arista Records. The label's first release, the album features production by Bad Boy founder Sean "Puffy" Combs, Easy Mo Bee, Chucky Thompson, DJ Premier and Lord Finesse, among others. Recording for the album took place during 1993-94 at The Hit Factory and D&D Studios in New York City. The partly autobiographical album tells the story of B. ...[truncated]

Going Back to Cali (The Notorious B.I.G. song) | "Going Back to Cali" is a song recorded by rapper The Notorious B.I.G. and Puff Daddy (uncredited) from the album "Life After Death". The song is noted as one of Biggie's popular songs from the album, well known for its catchy hook and beat. The song contains a sample of Zapp hit "More Bounce to the Ounce".

delta_p.trigger: Apply when summary_1 names a specific page title to verify (e.g., '10 Downing Street') and the agent role is to produce a single second-hop retrieval query to confirm a concise common-name claim.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep using the provided `question` and `summary_1` as input; preserve inclusion of the named page title tokens from summary_1 as the focal retrieval target.
delta_p.change_rule: Instead of emitting multiple site-filtered or separate-line queries, produce exactly one compact, single-line natural-language query. Begin with the canonical page title token(s) taken from summary_1, then append a short clarifying phrase that asks for the specific fact to verify (for example, a brief phrase asking what the location is commonly called in the United Kingdom). Do not include retrieval instructions, multiple queries, 'site:' filters, or additional commentary.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not output multiple queries or separate lines (no parallel '10 Downing Street' and 'Number 10' queries). Avoid adding site: filters, search operators, extra context, or the answer itself. Do not include meta-comments or instructions to the retriever.
delta_p.output_contract: Return a single-line string as the `query`. The line must start with the page title tokens from summary_1 and end with a concise natural-language verification phrase about the common UK name. No other text, no explanation, and no answer.
delta_p.why_local_transition: Combining the named page title with a short verification phrase focuses the retriever on the canonical page and the specific claim (common UK name), preventing noisy results caused by multiple separate or site-filtered queries.

match.transported_damage_hypothesis: The query agent will emit multiple separate/site-filtered or multi-line search strings instead of one compact, title-first verification query for the album, causing the retriever to return noisy, non-canonical pages and miss the album's release-date infobox.
match.expected_damaged_behavior: Retriever results omit or bury the canonical album page; the second-hop lacks the release-date evidence and the agent fails to produce the year (returns a clarification or no answer).
match.rationale: The W failure modifies how the second-hop query is formed: it turns a focused, title-anchored single-line verification query into multiple separate/site-filtered queries that invite noisy, irrelevant hits. The target C has the same structure (first hop names a specific album page and the agent's job is to emit a concise second-hop query to verify the release year). Transporting the query-shape failure is local to the query agent, does not require changing entities or answers, and plausibly produces the same failure mode (missed canonical page → missing release-year evidence) at the album-retrieval boundary.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are constructing a target-specific transported damaged output C_minus
for one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Inputs:
- C_plus is a correct current output/state for the target sample.
- W_minus -> W_plus is a matched W-side repair transition.
- The ordered W delta-p trace describes the repair behavior.

Task:
Infer the reverse W failure pattern and apply only that failure pattern to
the target sample's own visible inputs, producing a plausible LOCAL neighbor
C_minus of C_plus.

Strict rules:
- Produce an actual output for the selected agent boundary, not a prompt.
- Preserve the agent's native input/output contract.
- Use only visible_c_inputs as factual content.
- Never copy W-side entities, answers, titles, or sample-specific literals
  unless they independently occur in visible_c_inputs.
- Do not create unrelated corruption. Preserve enough useful C context for
  the result to remain a local neighbor.
- Damage one coherent behavior: weaken, remove, or blur an anchor, relation,
  qualifier, candidate distinction, evidence integration, output selection,
  or another matched failure pattern.
- An answer flip is not required and must not be claimed.
- Do not mention gold labels, oracle targets, evaluation metrics, source or
  target states, transport, prompt updates, or optimization in damaged_output.
- Do not wrap damaged_output in explanatory prose.

Agent:
query

Agent role:
Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

Base prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.
